compiled_state.title: next-batch ignores recorded file-impact: conflict detection reads title+comments only
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
next-batch never reads recorded file_impact; the documented conflict-aware scheduling contract is dead code (conflict seeds are scraped from title + comment text only).

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD, temp store)
1. Create an epic with two child tasks.
2. rebar set-file-impact <t1> '[{"path":"src/shared.py","reason":"edit"}]' and the same for <t2>.
3. rebar next-batch <epic> --output json -> BOTH tasks in the same batch, "files": [] each, skipped_overlap: [], conflict matrix all '.'.
4. Control: put the same backticked path in a COMMENT on each ticket -> conflict IS detected (SKIPPED_OVERLAP).

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected (CLAUDE.md + set-file-impact help): next-batch uses the recorded {path,reason} array to avoid scheduling file-conflicting tickets together.
- Actual: file_impact is never read by the scheduler; description handling is also dead (the ready_tasks dict drops it, so raw.get("description") is always None).

## Source
src/rebar/_engine/ticket-next-batch.sh — :627-634 ready_tasks dict (drops description/file_impact); :455-481 _load_ticket_body (title+comments only); :768 Candidate.files construction.

## Fix direction
Carry file_impact (and description) through the ready_tasks dict; union the recorded paths into Candidate.files. Exact validated diff in the implementation-notes comment on this ticket. Related: bug jaded-sled-pyre (element validation) keeps junk out of the same array.

## Suggested RED test
Two epic children with identical set-file-impact paths -> exactly one in batch, the other in skipped_overlap; no-file-impact case still batches both; JSON output keys unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Recorded file_impact paths participate in overlap detection (RED test first)
- [ ] No-impact tickets co-schedule as before; next_batch JSON schema keys unchanged
- [ ] test-ticket-next-batch-dispatcher.sh + reducer batch suites green

comments.body:
IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (fix mechanics experimentally validated in sandbox, 2026-06-09):
list --status=open,in_progress JSON ALREADY carries description and file_impact — the drop is purely in the ready_tasks projection. Validated minimal patch (two hunks in ticket-next-batch.sh):
1) In the ready_tasks.append dict (~:631): add
   "description": t.get("description", ""),
   "file_impact": t.get("file_impact", []),
2) In Candidate.__init__, AFTER the analyze_file_impact if/else (~:779), union declared paths into both branches:
   declared = {e["path"] for e in (raw.get("file_impact") or []) if isinstance(e, dict) and e.get("path")}
   if declared: self.files = set(self.files) | declared
Validated: identical set-file-impact on two epic children -> BATCH_SIZE 1, matrix X, SKIPPED_OVERLAP names conflict_file src/foo.py; no-impact case still batches both; --output json keys exactly match src/rebar/schemas/next_batch.schema.json (files/files_likely_read already in common.schema.json#/$defs/batch_item).
Regression suite: tests/scripts/test-ticket-next-batch-dispatcher.sh (also referenced by test-ticket-transition-deleted.sh).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: PLACEMENT CLARIFICATION (dry-run feedback): apply the file-impact union ONCE, immediately AFTER the analyze_file_impact if/else block closes in Candidate.__init__ (i.e., after the else-branch assignment of self.files/self.files_read), so both branches receive it — do NOT duplicate the union inside each branch. self.files is a set in both branches; set(self.files) | declared is type-safe.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

verify_commands.dd_text: identical recorded file-impact forces overlap skip; schema keys unchanged
